nodes.name: SessionRecord.swift
nodes.summary: SwiftData @Model for one completed or safe-worded play session (category, difficulty, prompts shown, duration) with a cascade-delete relationship to its RatingRecords.

nodes.name: SessionRecord
nodes.summary: SwiftData model persisting a single session's metadata and cascade-deleting its child RatingRecords; stores enums as raw strings to avoid SwiftData enum issues.

nodes.name: UserProfile.swift
nodes.summary: The central SwiftData @Model — one record per person created at onboarding's end; the durable source of truth for all onboarding/routing data, link state, and Desire Map completion.

nodes.name: UserProfile
nodes.summary: SwiftData model holding identity, onboarding routing (NMStage, AppMode, registers, archetype), link state and Desire Map flags; exposes derived linkState/defaultIntensity and preview fixtures.

nodes.name: DataStore.swift
nodes.summary: Central SwiftData persistence layer wrapping a ModelContext; every session/rating/streak/profile read and write goes through here. Also holds transitional RatingRecord/StreakRecord stub models.

nodes.name: DataStore
nodes.summary: Wraps a ModelContext to save sessions with ratings and streak bookkeeping, fetch sessions/ratings, fetch-or-create the streak and profile, and perform danger-zone deletions.

nodes.name: saveSession
nodes.summary: Persists a completed session: inserts the SessionRecord, creates a RatingRecord per reaction, and updates the streak record (extend/reset based on day continuity).

nodes.name: RatingRecord
nodes.summary: Transitional SwiftData @Model stub for a per-prompt rating tied to a SessionRecord, pending the post-migration replacement.

nodes.name: StreakRecord
nodes.summary: Transitional SwiftData @Model stub tracking streak and lifetime totals (current/longest streak, total sessions/prompts), pending the post-migration replacement.

nodes.name: ModelContext+Extensions.swift
nodes.summary: ModelContext extension adding saveWithLogging() — the project-wide replacement for silent try? context.save(), logging and rethrowing every failure via OSLog.

nodes.name: AppState.swift
nodes.summary: Root @Observable @MainActor app-level state owning the onboarding gate, link state, app mode, couple id and tab routing; the single writer of onboarding completion across truth/surface/cache.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses property didSet observers as the sole UserDefaults cache-write sites to keep the in-memory surface from desyncing with the durable UserProfile truth.

nodes.name: AppState
nodes.summary: Observable MainActor root state class hydrating from UserDefaults at init and exposing the only onboarding-completion writers (markOnboardingComplete/resetOnboarding/hydrateOnboardingState).

nodes.name: markOnboardingComplete
nodes.summary: The single completion writer — sets onboarding-complete on the UserProfile truth, saves the context, and flips the in-memory surface whose didSet writes the UserDefaults cache.

nodes.name: hydrateOnboardingState
nodes.summary: Reconciles the in-memory onboarding surface (and its cache) against the durable UserProfile truth at launch, correcting any drift from remote sync.

nodes.name: AuthService.swift
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor Sign in with Apple service over Supabase — handles nonce generation, the ASAuthorization delegate flow, session checks, sign-out, and guarantees a remote profile row on auth.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges the callback-based ASAuthorizationControllerDelegate into async/await by hopping back to the MainActor inside Task blocks.

nodes.name: AuthService
nodes.summary: Observable MainActor NSObject managing Apple sign-in and Supabase auth state (checkSession, signInWithApple, signOut), with SHA256-nonce helpers and a remote-profile guarantee.

nodes.name: checkSession
nodes.summary: Resolves real Supabase auth state at launch — signs out expired sessions, sets userId/isAuthenticated, and ensures the remote profile row exists. No simulator shortcuts.

nodes.name: ContentLoader.swift
nodes.summary: Static helper for reading bundled read-only JSON content (decks, cards, categories, desire items); all methods throw on failure rather than fatalError, with snake-case decoding.

nodes.name: ContentLoader
nodes.summary: Struct exposing generic single/array JSON decoders plus typed accessors (loadDeck, loadDesireItems, etc.) that throw ContentLoaderError on missing files or decode failures.

nodes.name: DesireSyncService.swift
nodes.summary: @MainActor service pushing private desire-map ratings to Supabase (batch upsert), invoking the compute-desire-matches edge function, and reading back client-safe matches and couple status.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a Sendable PendingDesireRating value snapshot taken before any await so a SwiftData @Model is never touched across a suspension point.

nodes.name: DesireSyncService
nodes.summary: MainActor singleton syncing desire ratings (resolving the profile id first), computing matches server-side, and fetching client-safe match rows and completion status for a couple.

nodes.name: syncRatings
nodes.summary: Maps local desire ratings to Supabase DTOs against the resolved profile id and batch-upserts them on (user_id, desire_item_id) so re-rating updates in place.

nodes.name: EntitlementService.swift
nodes.summary: Service layer for couple-level entitlements (Monetization M1) — reads the denormalized couples.access_tier and grants Core via the server-authoritative grant-entitlement edge function.

nodes.name: EntitlementService
nodes.summary: Injected Supabase service exposing fetchTier (RLS-scoped couple tier read) and grantCore (server-validated StoreKit JWS grant); never reads the service-role-only entitlements ledger.

nodes.name: LegalLinks.swift
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for the app's Terms and Privacy URLs (currently Apple placeholder pages) plus the LegalDoc enum that drives the legal-document sheet in the paywall and sign-in.

nodes.name: PairingService.swift
nodes.summary: Pure data-access layer for partner linking over Supabase — generates/claims pairing codes, fetches the linked partner identity, and polls (via realtime channel + timeout) until the couple forms.
nodes.languageNotes: pollForClaim races a realtime postgres-changes stream against a deadline timer using withThrowingTaskGroup, returning whichever resolves first.

nodes.name: PairingService
nodes.summary: Async/await Supabase pairing service: generateCode, claimCode (create-couple edge fn), fetchPartner (get-partner), and pollForClaim with realtime + deadline; errors always rethrown.

nodes.name: pollForClaim
nodes.summary: Subscribes to a per-user realtime channel and races a postgres-changes task against a deadline timer, returning the coupleId when the partner joins or throwing expiredCode on timeout.

nodes.name: generateCode
nodes.summary: Inserts a new pairing code row for the current user and reads back the DB-assigned expires_at so the waiting UI can count down and bound its poll.

nodes.name: ProfileService.swift
nodes.summary: @MainActor Supabase profile service — fetch-or-create the remote user_profiles row, scoped pairing-code lookups (name+pronouns only), identity/NM-stage updates, and profile-id resolution caching.

nodes.name: ProfileService
nodes.summary: MainActor service mapping the SupabaseProfile DTO and exposing fetchOrCreateProfile, lookupPairingCode (privacy-scoped), markOnboardingComplete, updateIdentity/updateNMStage, and ensureProfileExists.

nodes.name: fetchOrCreateProfile
nodes.summary: Selects the remote user_profiles row by auth_id and, if none exists, inserts a default profile and returns the created row (select-before-insert avoids duplicates).

nodes.name: ensureProfileExists
nodes.summary: Returns the cached or freshly-fetched Supabase profile id for an auth id (caching it in UserDefaults), throwing profileNotFound if no row exists; used by FK-bound writes.

nodes.name: RealtimeSessionService.swift
nodes.summary: Pure data-access transport for the two-device Curated Session over Supabase — curated_sessions CRUD, airlock column mutators, server-authoritative conditional advance, and a presence channel factory.
nodes.languageNotes: advance() uses a conditional UPDATE guarded on the expected current_index to make double-advance impossible across two devices.

nodes.name: RealtimeSessionService
nodes.summary: Async/await curated_sessions service: openSession, fetchOpenSession, per-column airlock mutators (bandwidth/consent/presence/status), and the conditional advance; UI/state owned elsewhere.

nodes.name: advance
nodes.summary: Server-authoritative card advance — increments current_index only if it still equals expectedIndex, returning whether this call moved the pointer or the partner already did.

nodes.name: sessionChannel
nodes.summary: Builds (without subscribing) the couple's realtime channel keyed by user id so presence joins/leaves are identifiable; the consumer owns the subscribe/track lifecycle.

nodes.name: StoreKitService.swift
nodes.summary: StoreKit 2 wrapper (Monetization M2) — loads the Core product, runs the purchase sheet returning a verified transaction + signed JWS, checks/restores ownership, and observes background transaction updates.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses StoreKit 2 VerificationResult pattern-matching to surface only verified transactions and their JWS for server re-verification; never grants on .unverified.

nodes.name: StoreKitService
nodes.summary: Service surfacing StoreKit 2 purchase/restore/ownership for the Core lifetime product and a detached Transaction.updates listener; state decisions delegated to EntitlementStore.

nodes.name: purchase
nodes.summary: Runs Apple's purchase sheet and returns a PurchaseOutcome carrying the verified transaction + JWS on success (finishing the transaction), or cancelled/pending/unverified.
